Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss And, Alternatively, Stay Action For Forum Non Convenies
Off Calendar Oct-24-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTs CLUB ACQUISITION COMPANY, JAMES BUTLER, ROB RIOS, R-MAC PROPERTIES, INC., SAN FELIPE BEACH CLUB S.A. DE C.V. and RANCHO EPOCAS DE ORO S.A. DE C.V.'s Motion To Dismiss And, Alternatively, Stay Action - The motion to dismiss or stay this action is denied. The mandatory forum selection clauses that defendants rely upon do not apply to this action. In the First Amended Complaint, plaintiff is suing for breach of the Transfer Agreement. The Transfer Agreement is an independent contract and it does not have a forum selection or Mexican choice of law clause. It is true that plaintiff's fraud and concealment causes of action refer to condominium unit 553 and do not directly reference the Transfer Agreement. Plaintiff, however, never executed an agreement regarding that unit with a forum selection or Mexican choice of law clause. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ehg
